headline: Week six: three summit mornings, a whisker shy of target
narrative: The number came in a whisker shy of target — close enough to call the volume done — but the shape drifted from the brief. The ask was a long mountain day; what happened instead was three solid summit mornings: a Higher Moor outing with hiking reps bolted on, a Mellor Moor reps session, and a Sunday trail run over Beacon Hill. Around them sat a club run, a parkrun run gratefully in the shade, and gym and rowing work to keep the engine turning. Time on feet finished a touch under the ask too — spreading the vert across shorter days is cheaper in hours than one big outing, and it shows.

The calf: silent again, six weeks running. That is the green light that matters as Base sharpens. Week seven is the block peak and asks for exactly what this week skipped — big days, and the first back-to-back: hike Saturday, descend Sunday. The weekend is the week now; arrange everything else around protecting it.
